title: Unify Bureau ready selection and live claims
goal: Connect deterministic ready eligibility, live thread focus, atomic claims, Grabowski resource leases, repository and PR conflict checks, isolated worktrees and durable closeout receipts into one collision-safe task pickup path.

acceptance.assertion: Task pickup requires registry state ready, satisfied dependencies, valid queue placement, no terminal state and no hard PR, task, repository or resource conflict.
The pickup path atomically records a live claim and acquires required Grabowski resource leases, or fails without leaving a partial claim.
Claim, heartbeat and release state remain outside Git and do not require a Bureau PR, while durable task definition, promotion and completion evidence remain reviewable registry truth.
A claimed repository mutation task receives one isolated worktree and any working PR binds exactly one valid task; ambiguous or conflicting states fail closed.
Expired or lost claims reconcile without duplicate execution, and successful closeout writes typed evidence, releases leases and removes terminal tasks from active lanes.
Status projection distinguishes no eligible work, eligible unclaimed work, claimed work and blocked work, so a low ready count is visible rather than silently treated as executor failure.

metadata.live_gap: The registry currently contains only one task in state ready despite many planned tasks; readiness, live focus and runtime leases are implemented in separate surfaces.

lease_scope_migration.rationale: Collision-safe pickup joins eligibility code, operational state, exact lease policy, worktree administration and task truth.
does_not_establish: safe mutation of terminal historical tasks
